raw_consumer_statement:
I am a XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  I am filing this formal complaint against Citibank for a direct violation of Regulation Z and a failure to uphold their contractual Zero Liability Policy regarding unauthorized transaction fraud. 
On XX/XX/year>, I discovered an unauthorized, fraudulent pending charge of {$59.00} on my account, masked with an XXXX billing description. I immediately called the card issuer 's fraud department to report that my card was compromised and to initiate an unauthorized transaction dispute. 

The frontline representative refused to accept the fraud report. She improperly attempted to classify an obvious authorization fraud event as a merchant dispute. She lectured me, stated that the issue was " my problem to fix, '' and informed me that I could not dispute the item while it was pending. Furthermore, she stated that I would have to monitor my account, call back after it posted, and enter a lengthy dispute process that " I may not win. '' Furthermore, the representative intentionally engaged in dangerous semantics to confuse the report. I explicitly and repeatedly requested to cancel the compromised plastic card and have a new card number issued. Instead of executing this standard security procedure, she repeatedly twisted my words, asking if I wanted to " close the account '' entirely. She weaponized this semantic runaround over and over throughout the conversation, causing unnecessary escalation and intentionally delaying critical security measures on a compromised account. 

By refusing to immediately flag a compromised card, block further unauthorized velocity, and queue the fraud dispute for settlement, the bank failed in its duty to protect the consumer. Furthermore, telling a consumer they " may not win '' a dispute on a completely unauthorized transaction explicitly undermines federal consumer protections. 

I have since locked the card myself to prevent further financial harm.
